blocks.description: Program header with change history (migration Phase 3), OPTIONS for macro debugging, LIBNAME for legacy (readonly source system), target (new platform), reconcil (validation results), and work; global migration parameters (%LET for batch_id, run_date, run_timestamp, tolerance, tol_type PCT/ABS, migration_rc, total_checks, passed_checks, failed_checks, n_tables=8, n_crit_tables=4, n_fin_tables=3, n_fk_pairs=5) and indexed table list macro variables (table1-table8: customers, accounts, transactions, products, orders, order_lines, suppliers, employees)
%INCLUDE for migration_utilities.sas shared migration utility macro library

%MACRO count_reconcile(table=, lib_src=, lib_tgt=): compares row counts between legacy source and migration target; two PROC SQL NOPRINT blocks capturing source and target counts into macro variables via INTO :src_count/:tgt_count; DATA step computing difference, percentage difference, and pass/fail status with CALL SYMPUTX updating global passed_checks/failed_checks counters; outputs to reconcil.count_result_&table; increments total_checks via %EVAL

%MACRO column_compare opening: documentation header, declaration with 4 parameters (table, key_cols, compare_cols, lib_src), %LOCAL variables, COUNTW for column/key counts, %DO loop dynamically building join condition string from key_cols using %SCAN

PROC SQL inside column_compare: FULL OUTER JOIN of source and target tables on dynamic join condition; COALESCE for key_value, CASE expression classifying rows as MISSING_IN_SOURCE/MISSING_IN_TARGET/MATCHED; %DO loop generating paired src_/tgt_ columns for each compare column; creates work._compare_&table

DATA step inside column_compare creating reconcil.col_compare_&table: ARRAY _src/*/ src_: and ARRAY _tgt/*/ tgt_: for paired column comparison; DO loop over DIM(_src) with NE check and VNAME for column identification; CATX for mismatch_detail with source/target values; handles MISSING_IN_SOURCE, MISSING_IN_TARGET, and matched-row column differences; outputs _ALL_ pass record when no mismatches found
PROC SQL NOPRINT inside column_compare: counts mismatches from reconcil.col_compare_&table excluding _ALL_ pass records; captures count into &n_mismatches macro variable
%IF/%ELSE inside column_compare: increments total_checks via %EVAL; if n_mismatches > 0 emits WARNING and increments failed_checks, else emits NOTE and increments passed_checks; %MEND column_compare

%MACRO sum_reconcile opening: documentation header, declaration with 4 parameters (table, key_cols, amount_cols, tolerance with default &tolerance), %LOCAL variables, COUNTW for amount/key column counts, %DO loop building dynamic join condition from key_cols

PROC SQL inside sum_reconcile: aggregates amounts by key columns from legacy source using %DO loops for dynamic SELECT (SUM with comma20.2 format) and GROUP BY column generation; creates work._sum_src_&table
PROC SQL inside sum_reconcile: aggregates amounts by key columns from migration target using %DO loops for dynamic SELECT and GROUP BY; creates work._sum_tgt_&table

DATA step inside sum_reconcile: MERGE of work._sum_src_&table and work._sum_tgt_&table with %DO loop RENAME (original to src_/tgt_ prefixed columns), dynamic BY statement; computes diff_&acol for each amount column via COALESCE; %IF &tol_type=PCT for percentage tolerance (pct_diff calculation, threshold comparison against &tolerance*100) %ELSE absolute tolerance (direct abs(diff) threshold); sets status to FAIL with breach_detail on tolerance breach, defaults to PASS; creates reconcil.sum_result_&table

Inside sum_reconcile: PROC SQL NOPRINT counting tolerance breaches (status=FAIL) into &n_breaches; increments total_checks; %IF n_breaches > 0 emits WARNING and increments failed_checks, %ELSE emits NOTE confirming amounts within tolerance; %MEND sum_reconcile

%MACRO referential_integrity(parent_table=, child_table=, fk_col=): PROC SQL creating reconcil.orphans_&child_table._&fk_col via LEFT JOIN of child to parent on FK column with WHERE p.&fk_col IS NULL and c.&fk_col IS NOT NULL; captures orphan count into &n_orphans; DATA step creating reconcil.fk_result_&child_table._&fk_col with parent/child/FK metadata, orphan_count, CALL SYMPUTX updating passed/failed counters; increments total_checks; %IF conditional for pass/fail messaging; %MEND
%MACRO generate_reconciliation_report opening: documentation header describing compilation of all reconciliation results into unified dashboard with PDF output; declaration, %LOCAL i, %PUT banner

DATA step inside generate_reconciliation_report: merges all count reconciliation results using %DO loop over &n_tables generating SET list of reconcil.count_result_&&table&i datasets; creates reconcil.all_count_results

DATA step inside generate_reconciliation_report: merges all FK validation results from 5 named datasets (reconcil.fk_result_orders_customer_id, order_lines_order_id, order_lines_product_id, transactions_account_id, employees_dept_id) with consistent LENGTH statement; creates reconcil.all_fk_results

PROC SQL inside generate_reconciliation_report: creates reconcil.migration_dashboard with batch_id, run_date, total/passed/failed counts from macro variables; CASE expression computing pass_rate percentage; nested CASE classifying migration_status as CERTIFIED (>=100%), CONDITIONAL (>=95%), REVIEW_REQUIRED (>=80%), or FAILED

DATA step inside generate_reconciliation_report creating reconcil.quality_score from reconcil.migration_dashboard: weighted scoring components (count 0.30, column 0.30, sum 0.25, fk 0.15); quality_score derived from pass_rate; IF/ELSE IF chain generating recommendation text (Proceed to go-live / Review and remediate / Re-run migration / Full re-migration required) based on score thresholds (100/95/80); scored_at timestamp

ODS PDF output setup inside generate_reconciliation_report: ODS PDF FILE= with journal style, PROCLABEL, TITLE1/TITLE2 with batch and date; PROC REPORT on reconcil.quality_score with 9 DEFINE columns (batch_id, run_date, total/passed/failed checks, pass_rate, quality_score, migration_status, recommendation); COMPUTE block with conditional CALL DEFINE styling migration_status red/bold for FAILED, green/bold for CERTIFIED

PROC REPORT inside generate_reconciliation_report on reconcil.all_count_results: TITLE3 'Row Count Reconciliation'; 6 DEFINE columns (table_name, src_count, tgt_count, diff, pct_diff, status) with comma12. and 8.4 formats; COMPUTE block highlighting FAIL rows with lightyellow background

PROC REPORT inside generate_reconciliation_report on reconcil.all_fk_results: TITLE3 'Referential Integrity Validation'; 5 DEFINE columns (parent_table, child_table, fk_column, orphan_count, status) with FAIL row highlighting; TITLE reset, ODS PDF CLOSE, %PUT confirmation, %MEND generate_reconciliation_report
%PUT messages: migration validation suite execution banner with batch_id, run_date, tolerance parameters; Phase 1 (Count Reconciliation) header

%MACRO run_count_checks with %DO loop iterating over &n_tables (8 tables): calls %count_reconcile(table=&&table&i) for each indexed table; includes %IF &syserr > 4 error check setting migration_rc=1 and emitting ERROR on system failure; %MEND run_count_checks
Invocation of %run_count_checks executing count reconciliation loop for all 8 migration tables

Phase 2 header and four %column_compare invocations for critical tables: customers (key=customer_id, compare=first_name last_name email phone status), accounts (key=account_id, compare=account_type balance open_date status), products (key=product_id, compare=product_name category unit_price active_flag), orders (key=order_id, compare=order_date customer_id total_amount status)

Phase 3 header and three %sum_reconcile invocations for financial tables: transactions (key=account_id, amounts=debit_amount credit_amount balance), orders (key=customer_id, amounts=total_amount tax_amount discount_amount), order_lines (key=order_id, amounts=line_total unit_price); all using &tolerance parameter

Phase 4 header and five %referential_integrity invocations: customers->orders (customer_id), orders->order_lines (order_id), products->order_lines (product_id), accounts->transactions (account_id), departments->employees (dept_id)

Phase 5 header and %IF &migration_rc=0 conditional: on success invokes %generate_reconciliation_report to compile results and generate PDF; on failure emits ERROR messages about skipping report generation with batch_id reference

DATA step creating reconcil.validation_log: persists final migration validation summary with batch_id, run_date, total/passed/failed counts; IF/ELSE setting status to ALL_PASSED or HAS_FAILURES; CATX building detail string with check breakdown; DATETIME() timestamp with datetime20. format
PROC DATASETS cleanup: deletes temporary work datasets matching _compare_:, _sum_src_:, _sum_tgt_: patterns with NOLIST NOWARN options
Final %PUT summary banner with migration validation completion status, batch_id, total_checks, passed/failed breakdown; OPTIONS reset to nomprint nomlogic nosymbolgen
